rule: For ad-hoc ops / world-touching tasks that bypass /feature (deploy/transport/connectivity/security/auth/control-topology), the alignment phase + the prior-art/existing-tooling check (Directive 1.7) apply too: state a one-line assumptions block or ask <=3 scoping questions + check for an existing tool BEFORE building.

observations.evidence: deploy/connectivity redirect chain (no-USB / convenient / generic / operate-freely / 'you missed the control topology') + the pre-existing agentvoice-pair tool the loop never checked for

rule: On each completed /feature loop AND any oscillating/abandoned/escalated or hand-coded-OUTSIDE-the-loop work, append a decisions.jsonl row (task-type / rounds / verdict incl. 'oscillation'|'abandoned'), so the tier table sees the HARD cases, not just clean wins — 'upgrade-fast on a struggle' is blind to anything unlogged.

observations.evidence: 3 /observe harvests found ZERO per-task outcome rows -> tier-learning had no data to read

the ~10-round voice/VAD oscillation (project's hardest task-type) left ZERO rows because it was hand-coded inline outside /feature; only clean wins were logged -> tier policy blind to the struggle (same-project repeat, not an independent vote)

promoted.note: HUMAN-promoted on edge-c2 (single-project) evidence, consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project threshold; judged as general framework hygiene, not project idiosyncrasy. Generalized (anecdotes stripped) on the way in.

rule: The tester must deliberately probe PAST documented/expected bounds (size/length/count/concurrency + permission/network/error edges) on any novel I/O path, not just the happy path.

observations.evidence: three 'audio PASS' verdicts shared a blind spot (all fixtures <1MiB) while a real >1MiB-drop bug only the reviewer reasoned past

TEMPORAL/STATEFUL extension: happy-path harness PASSed while real blockers hid past a limit/over time -- chat deque rolling past maxlen FROZE the display, a streamed partial line BLOCKED a read (defeating watchdog+timeout), a fault-in-fault spun infinitely, output rendered PAST terminal height cropped the newest line. Probe past buffer/maxlen, past timeout, during-error, over a long session; for TUI assert the rendered FRAME not widget state. (2nd edge-c2 obs -- not independent.)

promoted.note: HUMAN-promoted on edge-c2 (single-project) evidence, consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project threshold; judged as general framework hygiene, not project idiosyncrasy. Generalized (anecdotes stripped) on the way in.

rule: When a fix is hand-tuning a heuristic PARAMETER (threshold/window/timeout/retry/sensitivity) and >=2 rounds fail with the SAME failure mode, STOP: treat non-convergence as a WRONG-PRIMITIVE signal, run the Directive-1.7 prior-art scan for a robust replacement, and escalate to /feature -- don't keep patching the constant. The 2nd identical failure is the signal to change the primitive, not the parameter.

observations.evidence: energy-VAD tuned ~10 rounds (mic gain/RMS/silence-window/mic-swaps), same cutoff/hang across phone+desktop; one /feature run replaced the primitive (energy-VAD->Silero) and converged. Same shape thrice this project: raw-port->tailscale-serve, JSON-blob->SSE, energy-VAD->Silero.

4th wrong-primitive instance: hand-rolled rich.Live scroll + wrap-aware-tail math (termios/cell_len) -> switched to Textual native TabbedContent+RichLog, which DELETED the fragile code. (same project.)

promoted.note: HUMAN-promoted on edge-c2 (single-project) evidence, consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project threshold; judged as general framework hygiene, not project idiosyncrasy. Generalized (anecdotes stripped) on the way in.

rule: After proposing/scoping a feature, STOP and wait for an EXPLICIT go before writing code. Conceptual agreement, clarifying questions, or re-confirming the concept are NOT build confirmation. (Same act-before-validated family as scope-ops-prior-art; this is the next gate: scope -> explicit GO -> build.)

observations.evidence: built the claude-voice project-juggling feature before the user confirmed; user: 'I did not confirm it yet :) ... wait for explicit confirmation'. Recurs with the 2026-06-19 deploy redirect-chain (scope-ops-prior-art) -- same family, same project (not yet independent across projects).

rule: Before launching a KNOWN-HEAVY built-in fan-out (deep-research, large workflows), state the rough scale/cost up front and offer a capped/targeted run; treat hard session/rate limits as a spend guardrail; prefer resume+inline-synthesis to recover an interrupted run over re-spending. DISTINCT from dispatch.md 'never gate parallel WIDTH on a token estimate' (that rejects token-estimate as the parallelization-width gate) -- this is a spend-CAP + human-consent guardrail for expensive ops, not a width gate.

observations.evidence: deep-research spawned 112 agents / ~1.99M tokens, hit the Anthropic session rate limit mid-run, killing synthesis; user flagged 'insane amount of agents' and asked to estimate effort first. Recovered 16 verified claims via resume + inline synthesis at ~0 extra agents.

rule: In voice/TTS sessions, keep spoken turns short -- lead with the answer/outcome and push detail and citations to a file -- to avoid the channel truncating long responses.

observations.evidence: multiple 'you got cut off' / 'please repeat' from the user on long spoken turns during a TTS session; citation-heavy research summaries especially unsuited to speech.

rule: When a request carries redesign/cosmetic intent ('nicer visualization', 'aggressive cleanup of the UI/viz', 'make it look better', 'refactor the front end'), confirm cosmetic-vs-structural SCOPE up front. Do NOT default to a conservative structural/data-layer-only refactor that leaves the look unchanged -- surface the choice (data/logic refactor vs visual redesign) and get the user's pick before building, or you under-deliver and they flag the gap.

observations.evidence: user invited 'aggressive cleanup of the viz layer' + 'nicer visualization'; I refactored the data layer (structured reads, rapid-fire/classic split, perf) but preserved the look; user: 'so the UI is basically the same, right?' -- under-delivered on the cosmetic intent.

rule: Keep tester (black-box, real-data) and reviewer (code-reading, blind) DECORRELATED -- never collapse to one -- for TUI/terminal-render + failure-mode/concurrency + long-running/stateful work. The blind reviewer reliably catches reproduced blockers the happy-path black-box tester PASSes, because those bugs only manifest under a fault/race/render/past-a-limit. Inline hand-coding (skipping the reviewer) is exactly when such blockers ship.

observations.evidence: the rich->Textual TUI + streaming/heal/adaptive arc: the blind reviewer caught a REAL REPRODUCED blocker the black-box tester PASSed 6 separate times (stray-prints corrupting the alt-screen, TTS-self-fault infinite spin, adaptive command-loss+false-positive, streaming partial-line readline-block, Textual deque-rollover freeze, follow-active tab revert). 6 same-project occurrences = correlated, not 6 independent votes.

promoted.note: HUMAN-promoted on edge-c2 (single-project) evidence, consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project threshold; judged as general framework hygiene, not project idiosyncrasy. Generalized (anecdotes stripped) on the way in.

rule: For TUI/CLI work, the reward's 'real-data, human-reproducible' check must be satisfied at the FRAME level: drive the real binary under a PTY at a realistic size with representative input (long wrapping output, scroll, tab-switch, multi-tick long run) and ASSERT ON THE RENDERED SCREEN (newest line visible, tab switched+stuck, text wrapped not truncated, progress advancing) -- plus a MANDATORY human live smoke before 'done'. Widget/object-state assertions (e.g. Textual run_test reading .lines/.active) are blind to render/crop/scroll/wrap; pilot-green is necessary, never sufficient.

observations.evidence: the Textual TUI passed --selftest + run_test() Pilot (widget-state) yet shipped a string of bugs ONLY the human found by running it: chat desync, head-vs-tail clipping, wrap-rendered-as-horizontal-slider, tabs that don't switch/stick, no live progress, speech cut off. Directive-3 miss: tested the implementation's imagination, not real usage.

promoted.note: HUMAN-promoted on edge-c2 (single-project) evidence, consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project threshold; judged as general framework hygiene, not project idiosyncrasy. Generalized (anecdotes stripped) on the way in.

rule: (a) On a long-running DAEMON/TUI/server reported as 'hanging/odd', FIRST suspect a STALE in-memory build (the process doesn't reload edited files) -- verify the running PID started after the latest edit + check its event log before debugging code. (b) After a worker hands back, VERIFY git branch before the dispatcher commits; prefer 'git checkout <sha> -- <files>' over a blind cherry-pick when a commit's parent already holds most of the change.

observations.evidence: user repeatedly perceived hangs from an 8h-old in-memory desk-voice build; and a worker left HEAD on a stray branch so a commit landed there + a naive cherry-pick brought only the delta -> a broken commit + recovery round.

promoted.note: HUMAN-promoted on edge-c2 (single-project) evidence, consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project threshold; judged as general framework hygiene, not project idiosyncrasy. Generalized (anecdotes stripped) on the way in.

rule: The periodic /observe hook (e.g. the ~hourly UserPromptSubmit nudge) is a DO-IT obligation, not advisory. Run /observe proactively at each natural break (feature accepted/committed, stopping point) where it has fired since the last harvest; /reflect on real signal. Don't wait for the human to invoke the rituals -- the periodic harvest exists to catch inline-drift/testing-gaps/recurring-rounds EARLY. When a harvest reports 'healthy', also audit PROCESS adherence (did the rituals run on cadence? is the done-questionnaire used+complete?) -- a code-only harvest is blind to the dispatcher's own drift.

observations.evidence: the dispatcher ignored the ~hourly /observe nudge for a multi-day arc, running rituals only when the human invoked them -> 6 reviewer-saves + a string of human-found live bugs + a git mishap piled up before any reflection; the human had to be the watchdog ('you forgot to /observe and /reflect periodically'). A 'healthy -> no-op /reflect' then missed the cadence-drift signal itself.

promoted.note: HUMAN-promoted on edge-c2 (single-project) evidence, consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project threshold; judged as general framework hygiene, not project idiosyncrasy. Generalized (anecdotes stripped) on the way in.

rule: The shared done-questionnaire (worker fills, reviewer answers blind) must include an explicit REAL-USAGE TEST item (Directive 3): how was this exercised the way it's ACTUALLY used, and the gap to reality -- for UI/CLI the rendered frame at a real terminal + a mandatory human smoke; for long-running/stateful, past-the-limits + over-time. Without it, Directive 3 stays dispatcher prose and a green mock/state-check sails through; making it a questionnaire item both the worker and the decorrelated reviewer must answer FORCES the real-usage confrontation.

observations.evidence: the done-questionnaire (Feature/Built/Why/Weak-spots/Requirements/Open) had NO real-usage-test item; the Textual CLI passed it (widget-state) yet shipped a string of live bugs only the human found -- the testing discipline existed in CLAUDE.md/dispatch.md but wasn't an enforced question, so it was skipped.

promoted.note: HUMAN-promoted on edge-c2 (single-project) evidence, consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project threshold; judged as general framework hygiene, not project idiosyncrasy. Generalized (anecdotes stripped) on the way in.

rule: Observed DURATION is an orchestration under-use signal: if a single worker runs long (~>30-45min) or a task is estimated to, don't let it grind as one opaque call. A single-file WRITE still can't parallelize, but a long single-file task usually means the increment is too big (re-scope smaller) and/or you should fan out the parallelizable READS (prior-art, test harness, verification) and/or split into checkpointed worker->test rounds so the dispatcher stays in the loop. Compatible with 'never gate width on a token estimate' -- this is whether to decompose/re-scope/checkpoint, not estimating width. Long + opaque = re-structure, not endure.

observations.evidence: long opaque single-worker calls across the voice arc both under-used parallelism and were risky -- a worker DIED at the session limit mid-task and needed recovery; long black-box calls also defer all course-correction to one round where the reviewer then finds the blockers.

rule: In an NL/voice command router, forgiving fuzzy/prefix matching is safe for EXPLICIT-intent commands (the user clearly signaled the action) but DANGEROUS as the switch-vs-prompt discriminator for AMBIGUOUS natural phrasings (which might be a normal prompt). Split strictness by intent-explicitness: explicit -> forgiving resolve; natural/ambiguous -> STRICT exact/alias, else fall through (NEVER silently discard the utterance). A loose matcher doubling as an intent-classifier false-fires — and under --skip-permissions a false-switch runs commands against the wrong target.

observations.evidence: broadened 'go to/work on/continue' switch grammar fed partials into prefix+difflib(0.6) resolve_project: 'go to marketing'->market-pred, 'work on edge cases'->edge-c2, 'mistral to summarize'->mistral, 'e'->edge-c2; on wrong resolve the real prompt was silently dropped. Caught by decorrelated tester+reviewer (worker's green PTY+66/66 hid it). Fixed by per-branch strict gate; 72/72 + human-sim PTY.

rule: Any unattended/elevated agent MUST have a kill/abort path the user can trigger WITHOUT the spawned agent's cooperation (a control word intercepted before enqueue, an external signal) — not a prompt the running agent must choose to honor. A panicking user saying 'stop' must not have it re-fed to the runaway.

observations.evidence: edge-c2 2026-06-30: always-on voice agent ran `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions` with only a 4h timeout (no cost cap), blind `--resume` of a poisoned cross-project session, and no user kill path; a runaway burned ~93% of the user's quota and 'stop' was queued as a prompt that re-fed it.

rule: An agent that spends money/quota needs a COST/usage ceiling distinct from a wall-clock timeout. A long wall-clock bound (e.g. 4h) is not a spend cap — a runaway can exhaust the quota inside it. Cap tokens/$/turns, not just seconds.

observations.evidence: edge-c2 2026-06-30: always-on voice agent ran `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions` with only a 4h timeout (no cost cap), blind `--resume` of a poisoned cross-project session, and no user kill path; a runaway burned ~93% of the user's quota and 'stop' was queued as a prompt that re-fed it.

rule: Never blindly `--resume` a prior session. Before continuing, verify the session is the intended, on-task, same-project one — a stale/poisoned/foreign session resumed unconditionally will silently continue unrelated (possibly destructive) work.

observations.evidence: edge-c2 2026-06-30: always-on voice agent ran `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions` with only a 4h timeout (no cost cap), blind `--resume` of a poisoned cross-project session, and no user kill path; a runaway burned ~93% of the user's quota and 'stop' was queued as a prompt that re-fed it.

rule: always-on + `--skip-permissions`/`--dangerously-skip-permissions` is a standing security hazard that MUST hit the dispatch.md risk-floor (irreversible/security → deliberate risk review + human go) BEFORE going live — not graded only at the per-feature level. The per-task loop grades features; the system's blast radius needs its own review.

observations.evidence: edge-c2 2026-06-30: always-on voice agent ran `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions` with only a 4h timeout (no cost cap), blind `--resume` of a poisoned cross-project session, and no user kill path; a runaway burned ~93% of the user's quota and 'stop' was queued as a prompt that re-fed it.
